---
title: "Module_01_WKSP_02"
author: "Justin Proctor"
date: "2026-05-20"
output: html_document
---

#note this rmd file contains code from all workshops (1-4) in module 1 so that a knitted file of all workshops could be created.
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## MB5370 Module 1: Programming Fundamentals

## Workshop 1: Introduction/Foundations
```{r}
source("Module_01_WKSP_01.R")
```
## Workshop 2: Visualization using ggplot2

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r first plot with mpg dataset}
library(tidyverse)
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy))
```


```{r point color by class using the "mpg" dataset}
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy, colour = class))
```

```{r point size by class}
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy, size = class))
# Warning: Using size for a discrete variable is not advised. 
```

```{r color transparency by class}
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy, alpha = class))
```

```{r point shape by class}
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy, shape = class))
```

```{r blue points}
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy), color = "blue")
```

```{r when color equals display less than 5}
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy, color = displ < 5))
```

```{r using facet wrap}
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) + 
  facet_wrap(~ class, nrow = 2)
```

```{r using facet grid}
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) + 
  facet_grid(drv ~ cyl)
```

```{r replacing "drv" with "."}
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) + 
  facet_grid(. ~ cyl)
```

```{r display data as points}
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy))
```

```{r display data as smooth line}
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_smooth(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy))
```

```{r}
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
#  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) # points horrible 
  geom_smooth(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) # try smooth line
```

```{r separating cars into their drive type}
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_smooth(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy, linetype = drv, color = drv))
```

```{r setting group aesthetic}
 ggplot(data = mpg) +
  geom_smooth(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy, group = drv))
```

```{r changing color}
ggplot(data = mpg) +
  geom_smooth(
    mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy, color = drv),
    show.legend = FALSE,
  )
```

```{r multiple geoms}
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) +
  geom_smooth(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy))
```

```{r same as above with programmatic efficiency}
ggplot(data = mpg, mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  geom_smooth()
```

```{r different aesthetics in different layers}
ggplot(data = mpg, mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(color = class)) + 
  geom_smooth()
```

```{r filtering to plot only a subset of the data}
ggplot(data = mpg, mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(color = class)) + 
  geom_smooth(data = filter(mpg, class == "subcompact"), se = FALSE)
```

```{r coding exercise}
ggplot(data = mpg, mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  geom_smooth()

ggplot() + 
  geom_point(data = mpg, mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) + 
  geom_smooth(data = mpg, mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy))

#graphs look the same because conditons set are the same, just established at different points along the code
```

```{r bar chart utilizing "diamonds" dataset}
ggplot(data = diamonds) + 
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = cut))
```

```{r recreating above using stat count}
ggplot(data = diamonds) + 
  stat_count(mapping = aes(x = cut))
```

```{r overrididing a default stat using tribble function}
demo <- tribble(
  ~cut,         ~freq,
  "Fair",       1610,
  "Good",       4906,
  "Very Good",  12082,
  "Premium",    13791,
  "Ideal",      21551
)
demo

ggplot(data = demo) +
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = cut, y = freq), stat = "identity")
```

```{r displaying a bar chart of the proportion of total diamond dataset rather than count}
ggplot(data = diamonds) + 
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = cut, y = stat(prop), group = 1))
# Warning: `stat(prop)` was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
```

```{r showing more transparency on transformation details}
ggplot(data = diamonds) + 
  stat_summary(
    mapping = aes(x = cut, y = depth),
    fun.min = min,
    fun.max = max,
    fun = median
  )
```

```{r changing aesthetics of bar chart}
ggplot(data = diamonds) + 
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = cut, colour = cut))
ggplot(data = diamonds) + 
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = cut, fill = cut))
```

```{r changing color by clarity variable}
ggplot(data = diamonds) + 
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = cut, fill = clarity))
```

```{r position adjustments to customize plots by identity}
#To alter transparency (alpha)
ggplot(data = diamonds, mapping = aes(x = cut, fill = clarity)) + 
  geom_bar(alpha = 1/5, position = "identity")

#To color the bar outlines with no fill color
ggplot(data = diamonds, mapping = aes(x = cut, colour = clarity)) + 
  geom_bar(fill = NA, position = "identity")
```

```{r position adjustments to customize plots by fill}
ggplot(data = diamonds) + 
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = cut, fill = clarity), position = "fill")
```

```{r position adjustments to customize plots by dodge}
ggplot(data = diamonds) + 
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = cut, fill = clarity), position = "dodge")
```

```{r position adjustments to customize plots by jitter}
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy), position = "jitter")
```



<!-- TEMPLATE FOR USING GGPLOT: -->

<!-- ggplot(data = <DATA>) + -->
<!--   <GEOM_FUNCTION>( -->
<!--      mapping = aes(<MAPPINGS>), -->
<!--      stat = <STAT>, -->
<!--      position = <POSITION> -->
<!--   ) + -->
<!--   <FACET_FUNCTION> -->



```{r}
view(penguins)
```

```{r penguin count by species and island}
ggplot(data = penguins) + 
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = island, fill = species))
```

```{r penguin count by species and island with dodge positioning}
ggplot(data = penguins) + 
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = island, fill = species), position = "dodge")
```



## Workshop 3: Reproducible Research in MS: connecting R, Rstudio, Git, and GitHub

```{r}
#install.packages("usethis")
```

```{r}
library(usethis)

#use_git_config(user.name = "justin-proctor", user.email = "justinproctor24@gmail.com")
#commented out to avoid overriding existing git config
```



## Workshop 4: The Developer's Toolbox and AI

```{r}
#install.packages("ellmer")
#install.packages("chattr")
```

```{r}
#install.packages("palmerpenguins")
```

```{r using github copilot to pull penguins package and load it}
#load tidyverse and palmerpenguins libraries


library(tidyverse)
library(palmerpenguins)

# Create a ggplot scatter plot using the penguins dataset. 
# Plot bill_length_mm on the x-axis and bill_depth_mm on the y-axis.
# Color points by species and use geom_point with size 3.
# Add a minimal theme and clean labels.
ggplot(penguins, aes(x = bill_length_mm, y = bill_depth_mm, color = species)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Penguin Bill Dimensions",
       x = "Bill Length (mm)",
       y = "Bill Depth (mm)",
       color = "Species")

```

```{r open-ended comparative plotting using the penguins dataset}
#please create a boxplot comparing flipper_length_mm across different species in the palmerpenguins dataset. 
#place flipper length on the y axis and species on the x axis.
#facet these data by island and add a minimal theme with clean labels.
#please also use a custom color palette of your choice to differentiate the species in the boxplot.
ggplot(penguins, aes(x = species, y = flipper_length_mm, fill = species)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~ island) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Flipper Length by Species and Island",
       x = "Species",
       y = "Flipper Length (mm)",
       fill = "Species") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Adelie" = "#1f77b4", "Chinstrap" = "#ff7f0e", "Gentoo" = "#2ca02c"))
```



```{r testing chattr (chatgpt) plug in by asking it to produce the following code}
library(ggplot2)

data(iris)

ggplot(iris, aes(x = Sepal.Length, y = Sepal.Width, color = Species)) +
  geom_point()

```

```{r conditional logic - filtering data using base R}
# Simple Example: Checking Sea Surface Temperature (SST)
sst <- 30.2

if (sst > 29.5) {
  print("Warning: Marine heatwave threshold exceeded!")
} else {
  print("SST remains within baseline parameters.")
}
```

```{r conditional logic - filtering data using Tidyverse Alternative}
# Load tidyverse (if not already loaded)
library(tidyverse)

# Sample coral data
coral_monitoring <- tibble(
  site = c("Site_A", "Site_B", "Site_C"),
  depth_m = c(12, 35, 8)
)

# Classify depth using if_else
coral_monitoring <- coral_monitoring %>% 
  mutate(zone = if_else(depth_m > 30, "Deep Reef", "Shallow Reef"))

```

```{r conditional logic - filtering data using case_when()}
coral_monitoring <- coral_monitoring %>% 
  mutate(reef_category = case_when(
    depth_m < 10  ~ "Lagoon / Flats",
    depth_m <= 30 ~ "Crest / Slope",
    depth_m > 30  ~ "Mesophotic / Deep",
    TRUE          ~ "Unclassified" # Catch-all remainder
  ))
```

```{r automation with base R}
# Classic loop anatomy
for (year in 2020:2024) {
  print(paste("Processing climate data for year:", year))
}
```

```{r}
# Loop across index sequences
transect_lengths <- c(50, 100, 25, 75)

for (i in seq_along(transect_lengths)) {
  print(paste("Transect number", i, "measures", transect_lengths[i], "metres."))
}

```

```{r automation with tidyverse alternative - standard for-loop}
site_areas <- c(144, 400, 625)
results <- numeric(length(site_areas)) # Must build an empty container first

for (i in seq_along(site_areas)) {
  results[i] <- sqrt(site_areas[i])
}

results
```

```{r automation with tidyverse alternative - purrr}
library(purrr)
# Map directly and define your expected output type explicitly
mapped_results <- map_dbl(site_areas, sqrt)

```

```{r automation with tidyverse alternative - Iterating Across Complex Groups}
# Iterate a summary function over split lists of data
iris %>% 
  split(.$Species) %>% 
  map(~summary(.x))

```

```{r chatr task using purrr}
# List of vectors with fish counts
fish_counts <- list(
  lake1 = c(10, 12, 13, 9),
  lake2 = c(8, 6, 7),
  lake3 = c(14, 15, 13, 16, 15)
)

# For-loop to calculate the mean of each vector
fish_means <- numeric(length(fish_counts))
for (i in seq_along(fish_counts)) {
  fish_means[i] <- mean(fish_counts[[i]])
}
fish_means
```

```{r Function Construction Blueprint}
# Function Definition
calculate_coral_mortality <- function(initial_count, surviving_count) {
  
  # Logic safety switch using our conditional tools!
  if (initial_count <= 0) {
    stop("Initial count must be greater than zero.")
  }
  
  mortality_rate <- (initial_count - surviving_count) / initial_count
  return(mortality_rate)
}

# Utilizing your custom function
calculate_coral_mortality(initial_count = 120, surviving_count = 84)

```

```{r Exercise 4: Custom Function Construction Task}
convert_temp_c_to_f <- function(celsius) {
  if (celsius < -273.15) {
    stop("Temperature cannot be below absolute zero (-273.15°C).")
  }
 
  fahrenheit <- (celsius * 9/5) + 32
  return(fahrenheit)
}

# Valid test
convert_temp_c_to_f(25)
```
